name: Sillgratin (Herring & Potato Casserole)
recipeIngredient: 3 large Potatoes, peeled, cut into 1/8-inch slices
2 Matjes herring fillets, cut into 1/2-inch diagonal slices
1 tbsp Butter, cut into tiny bits
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 400°F.
2. Place potato slices in a bowl of cold water to prevent them from discoloring.
3. Heat 2 tablespoons of butter in a small frying pan and, when the foam subsides, add the sliced onions, cooking until they are soft and translucent but not brown. Set aside.
4. Choose a 1 to 1 1/2 quart baking dish attractive enough to bring to the table. Using a pastry brush or paper towels, spread with the remaining 1/2 tablespoon of butter. Drain potatoes and pat dry.
5. Arrange alternate layers of potatoes, herring, and onions in the baking dish, ending with a layer of potatoes. Season each layer lightly with pepper, sprinkle the top with breadcrumbs, and dot with bits of butter.
6. Pour in the cream, bring to a boil on top of the stove, then bake for 1 hour in the center of the oven until the potatoes are tender when pierced with the tip of a sharp knife or skewer. Serve directly from the baking dish.
